narrative:
Ample sunshine. Pleasant temperatures. No threat of rain. These are today’s highlights for a nice start to the weekend. Father’s Day will be just as nice, except warmer. That’s right on cue with the official start to summer, which is 4:24AM EDT on Sunday. Shower and thunderstorm potential returns to the mountains on Monday.

2027 Blue Ridge Calendar Pre-Publication Sale is Underway!

The 2027 Blue Ridge Calendar is being printed now and will be available around July 1. Until then, the Pre-Publication Sale is underway with the best price we will offer all year. Get your 2027 calendars at 26% off when you buy 2 or more. As in previous years, we have scheduled special Pickup times around NW NC to save you the cost of shipping. Click here for pickup times and locations.

You can see all the winning photography at our Photo Contest page .

Forecast Discussion

High pressure is in control of our weather today. Mid-and-high level moisture will generate some cloudiness, but it won’t detract from the pleasant temperatures and humidity levels.

On Sunday, a warm front passes through with little fanfare, other than to give temperatures a boost for Dad.

Changes occur on Monday as a cold front approaches from the northwest. This will result in fairly widespread coverage of showers and thunderstorms, mostly during Monday afternoon and evening.

The front sags to the south and will become stationary over the interior Southeastern United States for Tuesday and Wednesday, promoting drier and slightly cooler conditions.

The next front will be inbound for Thursday, slipping just to our south and east by Friday. That will represent the next opportunity for widely-scattered showers and thundershowers.

Detailed forecast discussion and analysis is only available to RaysWeather supporters.

Jun 18, 2026
Yadkin Valley Deluge

A morning complex of thunderstorms moving across the Ohio Valley and into West Virginia and Virginia produced an outflow boundary that pushed southward into North Carolina during the afternoon of June 18. This outflow boundary, the result of a cool rush of air from thunderstorms occurring to the north, served as an effective focusing mechanism for training thunderstorms that occurred over parts of Surry, northern Wilkes and southern Alleghany County during Thursday afternoon and evening.

This occurred under the backdrop of increasing tropical moisture returning from the southwest as the remnants of Tropical Storm Arthur moved into Alabama.

The result was excessive rainfall, in the range of 2-4 inches, with locally higher amounts of around 5 inches in central Surry County.

Jun 19, 2026
Monday Message for June 15, 2026

It's the pre-summer solstice edition of the Monday Message, available for subscribers. El Niño has officially arrived, and will have significant implications for our weather heading into fall and winter.
